metadata.title:
v. v. P. CHOSE
A CHEAPER


PLAN

PREFERRED SEEKING FAVORS
AT WASHINGTON TO DEAL
ING WITH LAND


OWNERS.

pages.raw_text:
Attorney Frank T. Post, chief
counsel for the Washington Water
Power company. is more concerned
at the present moment with the
success of the condemnation suits
started by the company against
the owners of lands abutting on
Occur dAlene lake than he is with
the suit of the government to Gust
the company. If the government
suit is lost by the company it can
still have recourse to the right of
eminent DOMAIN in the condemna.
ton of the desired low lands, if its
plea is sustained by the court.


The case at issue, which is being
made as a test before Judge Dunn
in the court at Occur d'Alene, is
that Of Charles Waters. The de-
Tense is insisting that the Wash
ington Water Power company, in
is operations in Idaho, is not a pub
lie utility corporation as defined
under the Idaho statute The case
has been exhaustively discussed in
court. and on the submission OF
briefs will be ready for a decision.


Spokane lawyers say that had the
Washington Water Power company
followed the plan of condemnation
in the beginning, when land was
cheaper. it would have gotten away
for much less money than it will
at the present time But the COM
pany chose the cheaper plan of
getting special favors from Wash
ington. which the government is
now seeking to revoke.
